headline: Lightning Network is a decentralized peer-to-peer protocol with no centralized deployments or admin addresses to catalog
short_headline: Decentralized P2P protocol; no admin addresses

findings.text: Lightning Network is not a smart contract protocol; it uses Bitcoin's native multisig scripting for 2-of-2 payment channels. No named contracts appear on block explorers under a protocol name tag.
No centralized deployments document exists. Payment channels are peer-to-peer multisig addresses created on-demand between parties, not pre-deployed contracts.
No smart contract audits apply. Lightning protocol security is specified via BOLT specifications and verified through community review and formal verification studies.

Multiple open-source implementations exist (LND, Core Lightning, Eclair, LDK) but these are node software, not deployed contract addresses. Each implementation is a standalone client for the decentralized protocol.
Not applicable—Lightning Network has no deployable smart contracts, admin addresses, or multi-chain instances. It is a single protocol specification implemented by distributed client software.

rationale.verdict: Grade is unknown because Lightning Network is a decentralized peer-to-peer protocol specification, not a smart contract-based DeFi protocol. It has no centralized contract addresses, governance tokens, treasury multisigs, or deployment artifacts to discover. The protocol is governed through open BOLT specifications maintained on GitHub with community consensus and implemented by independent node software (LND, Core Lightning, Eclair, LDK). Payment channels are ephemeral 2-of-2 multisig addresses between peers, not pre-deployed protocol infrastructure.

evidence.shows: Official Lightning Network homepage describing it as a decentralized network using smart contract functionality for instant payments via payment channels
Documentation page listing only whitepaper, technical summary, and presentation slides—no deployment registry or address book
Lightning Network Daemon is a complete node implementation, not a deployed smart contract
Core Lightning (c-lightning) is a standalone implementation in production since early 2018; it is node software, not contract addresses
Eclair is a Scala implementation of the Lightning Network protocol following BOLT specifications
LDK/rust-lightning is a modular Lightning protocol implementation in production since 2021; implements BOLT specifications
BOLT specifications are community-driven; pull requests welcome via delving bitcoin forum and lightning-dev mailing list

unknowns: D1: Lightning Network is a protocol specification, not a smart contract system; block-explorer name-tag search not applicable.
D2: No centralized deployments registry exists; payment channels are peer-to-peer creations, not protocol-owned contracts.
D3: No smart contract audits; protocol security analyzed via BOLT peer review and formal verification (arXiv:2505.15568).
D4-D8: Not applicable—no contract deployments, factories, or admin addresses to enumerate.

protocol_metadata.about: The Lightning Network is a decentralized peer-to-peer payment channel protocol built on Bitcoin. It enables off-chain instant micropayments and scalable transactions by allowing two parties to transact without broadcasting every transaction to the blockchain, settling only the final balance on-chain. The protocol is governed through open BOLT specifications with multiple independent implementations including LND, Core Lightning, Eclair, and LDK.
